title: Patrick A. Molligo

I study education, workforce development, and public policy, with particular attention to vocational training and the G.I. Bill.

title: Patrick A. Molligo
The Economic Effects Of Trade Policy Uncertainty
text: Download PDF

title: The Economic Effects Of Trade Policy Uncertainty
text:
Abstract
This paper studies the effects of unexpected changes in trade policy uncertainty (TPU) on the U.S. economy. Three measures of TPU are constructed using newspaper coverage, firms’ earnings calls, and tariff rates. Firm-level and aggregate macroeconomic data reveal that increases in TPU reduce business investment. The empirical results are interpreted through the lens of a two-country general equilibrium model with nominal rigidities and firms’ export participation decisions. News and increased uncertainty about higher future tariffs reduce investment and activity.

title: The Economic Effects Of Trade Policy Uncertainty
text:
Results
New measures of trade policy uncertainty are constructed using firm-level and aggregate data.
Trade policy uncertainty reduced U.S. investment by about 1.5% in 2018.
Open-economy DSGE Model with heterogeneous firms and nominal rigidities analyzes news and uncertainty about future tariffs.

title: The Economic Effects Of Trade Policy Uncertainty
text:
Citation
Caldara, Dario, Matteo Iacoviello, Patrick Molligo, Andrea Prestipino, and Andrea Raffo. “The economic effects of trade policy uncertainty.” Journal of Monetary Economics 109 (2020): 38-59.

title: The G.I. Bill and Vocational Training
text: Download PDF

title: The G.I. Bill and Vocational Training
text:
Abstract
The educational provisions of the World War II and Korean War G.I. Bills dramatically expanded access to postsecondary education and training. This paper examines their effects on vocational-school attendance and completion.
Replace this paragraph with the current abstract from the paper. A paper-page abstract can be longer than the short description used on the Research page.

title: Research
text:
My research lies at the intersection of labor economics, economic history, and the economics of education. I study how public policy and educational institutions shape human-capital investment and labor-market outcomes.







  

    
      
        Job Market Paper
      

      
        2026
      
    

    
      The G.I. Bill and Vocational Training
    

    

    

    
      This paper studies how the educational provisions of the World War II and Korean War G.I. Bills affected vocational training, educational attainment, and long-run labor-market outcomes.
